2604.13262.title: Rethinking Uncertainty in Segmentation: From Estimation to Decision
2604.13262.paper_summary: In medical image segmentation, uncertainty estimates are often reported but rarely used to guide decisions. We study the missing step: how uncertainty maps are converted into actionable policies such as accepting, flagging, or deferring predictions. We formulate segmentation as a two-stage pipeline, estimation followed by decision, and show that optimizing uncertainty alone fails to capture most of the achievable safety gains. Using retinal vessel segmentation benchmarks (DRIVE, STARE, CHASE_DB1), we evaluate two uncertainty sources (Monte Carlo Dropout and Test-Time Augmentation) combined with three deferral strategies, and introduce a simple confidence-aware deferral rule that prioritizes uncertain and low-confidence predictions. Our results show that the best method and policy combination removes up to 80 percent of segmentation errors at only 25 percent pixel deferral, while achieving strong cross-dataset robustness. We further show that calibration improvements do not translate to better decision quality, highlighting a disconnect between standard uncertainty metrics and real-world utility. These findings suggest that uncertainty should be evaluated based on the decisions it enables, rather than in isolation.
2604.13262.paper_summary_zh: 在醫學影像分割中，不確定性估計經常被報告，但很少用來指導決策。<lb/>我們研究缺失的步驟：如何將不確定性圖轉換為可行的政策，例如接受、標記或延遲預測。<lb/>我們將分割形式化為一個兩階段的流程，首先是估計，然後是決策，並顯示僅優化不確定性無法捕捉到大多數可實現的安全增益。<lb/>使用視網膜血管分割基準（DRIVE、STARE、CHASE_DB1），我們評估了兩種不確定性來源（蒙特卡羅隨機失活和測試時增強）結合三種延遲策略，並引入了一個簡單的信心感知延遲規則，優先考慮不確定和低信心的預測。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，最佳的方法和政策組合在僅25%的像素延遲下消除了高達80%的分割錯誤，同時實現了強大的跨數據集穩健性。<lb/>我們進一步顯示，校準改進並不轉化為更好的決策質量，突顯了標準不確定性指標與實際應用之間的脫節。<lb/>這些發現表明，不確定性應根據其促成的決策進行評估，而不是孤立地進行評估。
2604.13262.author: Saket Maganti et.al.
